Correlations of directly measured blood glucose levels with 3 glycemic biomarkers⺽HbA1c, glycated albumin, and fructosamine were progressively weaker with advanced CKD stages (G4″⺚G5), especially kidney failure treated by dialysis.

text: condition among patients with proteinuria, malnutrition, or treated by peritoneal dialysis. <sup>228</sup>

CGM and self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG) yield direct measurements of interstitial and blood glucose, respectively, that are not known to be biased by CKD or its treatments, including dialysis or kidney transplant
disagreement_detail: F:'The GMI can be derived from CGM that is performed with results either blinded to the patients during monitoring (“professional” version) or available to the patient in real time'

text: If it is a clinical concern that HbA1c may be yielding biased estimates of long-term glycemia
disagreement_detail: C:'HbA1c' vs F:'If it is a clinical concern that HbA1c may be yielding biased estimates of long-term glycemia'

text: For example, if HbA1c is lower than a concurrent GMI measure, the HbA1c can be interpreted to underestimate average blood glucose by the difference in measurements
disagreement_detail: C:'HbA1c' vs F:'For example, if HbA1c is lower than a concurrent GMI measure, the HbA1c can be interpreted to underestimate average blood glucose by the difference in measurements'

text: GMI may be useful for patients with advanced CKD, including those treated with dialysis, for whom reliability of HbA1c is low
disagreement_detail: C:'HbA1c' vs F:'GMI may be useful for patients with advanced CKD, including those treated with dialysis, for whom reliability of HbA1c is low'

text: In these situations, GMI needs to be re-established regularly
Daily glycemic monitoring with CGM or self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG) may help prevent hypoglycemia and improve glycemic control when glucose-lowering therapists are used.
disagreement_detail: C:'Daily' vs F:'Daily glycemic monitoring with CGM or self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG) may help prevent hypoglycemia and improve glycemic control when glucose-lowering therapists are used.'

text: Since sampling is performed intermittently, episodes of hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia are often harder to detect
Minimally invasive subcutaneous sensors which sample interstitial glucose at regular intervals (e.g., every 5–15 min).
Refers to sensors transmitting and/or displaying the data automatically throughout the day, so that the patient can review glucose levels and adjust treatment as needed
Provides a measure of average blood glucose levels calculated from CGM readings, expressed in units of A1C (%), that can be used to gauge whether clinical A1C levels are falsely high or low
disagreement_detail: C:'A1C' vs F:'Provides a measure of average blood glucose levels calculated from CGM readings, expressed in units of A1C (%), that can be used to gauge whether clinical A1C levels are falsely high or low'

text: hypoglycemic medications such as insulin. For daily glycemic monitoring, CGM and SMBG are frequently used but relatively high-cost options to assess real-time blood glucose.
disagreement_detail: B:'insulin' vs C:'daily' vs F:'hypoglycemic medications such as insulin. For daily glycemic monitoring, CGM and SMBG are frequently used but relatively high-cost options to assess real-time blood glucose.'

text: Advanced CKD substantially increases the risk of hypoglycemia in patients with diabetes treated by many oral agents and insulin.
disagreement_detail: B:'insulin' vs F:'Advanced CKD substantially increases the risk of hypoglycemia in patients with diabetes treated by many oral agents and insulin.'

text: CGM was not generally available for clinical use at that time (2007), and the potential advantages of the latter may make it preferable to SMBG among patients in whom daily monitoring is desired.
disagreement_detail: C:'daily' vs F:'CGM was not generally available for clinical use at that time (2007), and the potential advantages of the latter may make it preferable to SMBG among patients in whom daily monitoring is desired.'

text: In the judgment of the Work Group, there is no clear advantage of CGM or SMBG for patients with diabetes and CKD treated by oral glucose-lowering agents that do not cause hypoglycemia.

Factors guiding decisions on individual glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) targets.
disagreement_detail: C:'HbA1c' vs F:'Factors guiding decisions on individual glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) targets.'

text: Data from RCTs support the recommendation of targeting an individualized HbA1c level of <6.5% to <8.0% in patients with diabetes and CKD, compared with higher HbA1c targets.
disagreement_detail: C:'HbA1c' vs F:'Data from RCTs support the recommendation of targeting an individualized HbA1c level of <6.5% to <8.0% in patients with diabetes and CKD, compared with higher HbA1c targets.'

text: A systematic review with 3 comparisons examining the effects of lower ( ₆.0%, ⅽ 6.5%, and ⅼ 6.0%) versus higher (standard of care) HbA1c targets in patients with diabetes and CKD was undertaken.
